name: feature-validate-lean
description: Issue #76: swaps the stage-feature-validate task's railroaded step enumeration for a lean outcome-oriented block. The artifact contract, phase protocol, and honesty rule are OUTSIDE the replaced text and identical in both arms — this measures instruction STYLE (prescriptive steps vs stated outcomes), which is the de-railroading hypothesis. Adopt the lean style only on a measured composite-score win.

replacements.find: Follow these steps exactly. Step 1.1: Append the phase marker for read-dev-context (index 0). Step 1.2: Read .nightgauge/pipeline/dev-7600.json. Step 1.3: Parse the files_changed object with jq and confirm every listed file exists on disk. Step 1.4: Confirm tests_status.failed is 0; if it is not, note that for the summary. Step 2.1: Append the marker for build-verification (index 1). Step 2.2: Run npm run build and capture the exit code. Step 2.3: If the exit code is non-zero, set the build gate result to catch and copy the first error line into error_summary; otherwise set it to pass. Step 3.1: Append the marker for run-tests (index 2). Step 3.2: Run npm test and capture the exit code. Step 3.3: Map the exit code to the test gate result exactly as in Step 2.3. Step 4.1: Append the marker for lint-gate (index 3). Step 4.2: Run npm run lint and capture the exit code. Step 4.3: Map the exit code to the lint gate result exactly as in Step 2.3. Step 5.1: Append the marker for write-validate-context (index 4). Step 5.2: Assemble the validate-7600.json object field by field in the order given in the artifact contract. Step 5.3: Write it to disk, then verify with test -s that it is non-empty and with jq . that it parses. Step 5.4: Append one JSONL line per gate to .nightgauge/health/gate-metrics.jsonl using printf, one object per line. Step 6.1: Append the marker for summary (index 5). Step 6.2: Print a summary listing each gate and its result. Step 6.3: Re-run the test -s verification from Step 5.3 one final time before finishing.
replacements.replace_with: Verify the dev handoff against the repository, run the three gates (npm run build, npm test, npm run lint), and produce the artifacts. The phase log must show all six phases in order, and validate-7600.json must be non-empty, parseable JSON — verify that before finishing.
